name: Caminha / Porto de Recreio de Seixas
approachNotes: Minho-mouth bar ~1400 m, exposed to W/SW swell. Approach via Barra Sul between Insua islet and Ponta Ruiva. No charted channel marks - local knowledge strongly recommended. Max draft 2.5 m; tidal range ~2 m springs. Capitania signal station near Ponta do Cabedelo issues mandatory instructions. Ongoing silting; bar shifts seasonally. PT/ES border on the river centreline.
swellSensitivity: high - W/SW swell makes the bar dangerous
nearestTown: Caminha (500 m); basics; Minho-line train (Porto-Valenca) ~400 m.
orcaNotes: Offshore N Portugal has had orca interactions; risk lower inshore.
notes: Northernmost Portuguese refuge, just inside the Spanish border. Only ~12 fluvial berths at Seixas - very limited for offshore yachts. The Minho bar has claimed vessels - do NOT enter in onshore swell or at LW.

name: Marina de Viana do Castelo
approachNotes: River Lima entrance. Keep outside the 20 m contour from N or S to avoid rocky ledges. Entrance dredged to 8 m - passable all weathers/tides. At night use rear leading light on 005. Bar builds between outer-mole end and first port-hand buoy; in heavy weather keep 200 m off the breakwater end and enter from S. Spring ebb + river outflow tricky; obstruction ~2.5 m below LWS - deep-draught wait for tide. Channel buoyed inside; pass fishing harbour to port to the Eiffel-design steel bridge.
swellSensitivity: low-moderate - sheltered by breakwater
nearestTown: Viana do Castelo (10 min) - full city services; Minho-line trains to Porto ~1.5 h; Porto airport ~65 km.
orcaNotes: Offshore N Portugal orca zone in season.
notes: Best marina in N Portugal. 12.5 t crane, tidal grid, slipway. Blue Flag. Good weather-window base. Spring range 3 m - factor into timing.

name: Marina da Povoa de Varzim
approachNotes: Harbour entered on 045. From N give the N breakwater wide clearance - waves break at its extremity in any swell. Green buoys mark shallows near the breakwater. 21 nm S of Viana, 14 nm N of Leixoes. Depths 1.6-3.0 m inside; 4 visitor berths have min 3 m. Exposed breakwater head dangerous in heavy NW swell.
swellSensitivity: moderate - NW swell breaks at breakwater head
nearestTown: Povoa de Varzim (adjacent) - resort, supermarkets; Porto Metro line B (30-40 min to Porto); Porto airport ~40 km.
orcaNotes: Offshore N Portugal orca zone in season.
notes: Busy summer resort marina; adequate overnight between Viana and Leixoes; prefer Leixoes in bad weather.

name: Marina Porto Atlantico (Leixoes)
marinaHours: 09:00-12:30, 14:00-18:30 daily (else berth on reception pontoon, immediate left on entry)
approachNotes: Inside the large commercial harbour of Leixoes, 14 nm S of Povoa, ~2 nm N of the Douro. Outer commercial port entered in any conditions - big breakwaters give full protection. In W winds, strong swell + spray at the entrance. HEAVY commercial ship traffic - monitor VHF 16, keep clear. Proceed past the oil jetty to starboard; reception pontoon to port on entry. 5.5 t crane.
swellSensitivity: low - well inside commercial port
nearestTown: Leca da Palmeira/Matosinhos (adjacent); Porto 8 km - Matosinhos Sul Metro (line A) 10 min walk; Porto airport 8 km (direct Metro).
orcaNotes: Offshore N Portugal orca zone in season.
notes: Best all-weather port for Porto crew changes - can be entered in all conditions. Run by three sailing clubs; customs contact available.

name: Douro Marina (Marina da Afurada)
region: Portugal / Norte (Vila Nova de Gaia / Porto)
approachNotes: The Douro bar is potentially the most dangerous entrance on this coast in adverse conditions. N side protected by a long breakwater (red/white banded light). From S pass along the detached W breakwater, 100 m off until the entrance. Stay in the marked fairway - sandbanks to starboard. W wind + ebb + river outflow = steep breaking seas; port may close. Call marina VHF 09 before entry; enter near HW. Marina 1.5 nm upriver on the S bank.
swellSensitivity: high at bar - estuary well sheltered once inside
nearestTown: Afurada (adjacent, walkable); Porto centre 6 km by taxi.
orcaNotes: Offshore N Portugal orca zone in season.
notes: Largest marina between Cascais and Galicia; very good facilities. The Douro bar is the key risk - do not underestimate in NW swell or on the ebb.

name: Marina Clube da Gafanha (Aveiro / Barra)
approachNotes: WARNING: the Aveiro Barra is one of Portugal's most dangerous river-bar entrances. Channel between the W breakwater and an E dike, oriented 335, heads ~550 m apart; follow the W pier ~50 m off. Strong tidal currents; marina entrance narrow (~20 m). In any onshore wind/swell, especially on the ebb, the bar produces dangerous breaking seas and can close. Do NOT enter before half-flood, daylight only. Barra bridge 14 m clearance at HW. Aveiro harbour control VHF 11 broadcasts bar conditions. Ria de Aveiro lagoon very shallow (draft ~1 m).
swellSensitivity: extreme - dangerous bar in any onshore/swell
nearestTown: Gafanha da Encarnacao (adjacent); Aveiro city 8 km by bus; Aveiro rail (Porto 1 h, Lisbon 2.5 h).
orcaNotes: Offshore Centro Portugal orca zone in season.
notes: Beautiful lagoon town but the bar is the defining hazard. Only approach in settled conditions with correct tide. Ria interior excellent for shoal-draft cruising.

name: Marina da Figueira da Foz
approachNotes: WARNING: dangerous bar - has caused groundings and fatalities. Entrance dredged to min 7 m but swell builds in any onshore wind; in strong onshore gales huge standing waves form and the port closes. Enter on 073 between breakwaters. Arrive in the last 2 h of rising tide (flood counteracts river outflow). On the ebb (esp springs) cross-currents/eddies at marina entrance; spring ebb to 6 kt in winter. Bar conditions on VHF 11; entry permission VHF 16; berthing VHF 09. Large commercial fishing port.
swellSensitivity: extreme - bar closed in heavy onshore swell
bestTideEntry: Last 2 h of rising tide; avoid ebb in onshore conditions
nearestTown: Figueira da Foz (adjacent) - beach resort, supermarkets; rail to Coimbra B 1 h then Alfa Pendular.
orcaNotes: Offshore Centro Portugal orca zone in season.
notes: Important waypoint; well-sheltered once inside (350 berths). The bar is the critical challenge - monitor VHF 11. Base for Coimbra (30 min by train).

name: Nucleo de Recreio do Porto da Nazare
approachNotes: Unusually, Nazare can be entered at virtually any tide and in almost any weather - the basin is protected by its configuration. From N: dramatic Sitio promontory (105 m) 1 nm N of the harbour. The Nazare Canyon amplifies long-period NW swell (world-record big waves at Praia do Norte, 1 nm N) - keep well clear of Praia do Norte. In big NW swells visitor pontoons may surge. Facility reportedly run-down in recent reports.
swellSensitivity: moderate - basin protected but big NW swell surges; Praia do Norte extremely dangerous
nearestTown: Nazare (adjacent) - resort, fish market; buses to Caldas da Rainha (30 min) then train; Lisbon 1.5 h drive.
orcaNotes: Offshore Centro Portugal orca zone in season.
notes: Limited berths (52); facility condition criticised. Worth a stop for scenery and fish. Never sail close inshore between Nazare and Praia do Norte in any NW swell.

name: Marina de Peniche (Ribeira)
approachNotes: Peniche peninsula; marina (Ribeira basin) inside the W jetty. Fair approach, good channel marking, negligible current/tidal flow in the basin. Max draft 4 m (chart verify). Pump-out, crane, ramp. Fishing-vessel wash at the entrance. The Berlengas (nature reserve, dangerous rocks) lie 6-10 nm W - ensure well clear on the approach.
swellSensitivity: low - good protection
nearestTown: Peniche (2 min) - fishing town, fish market, supermarkets; express buses to Lisbon (1.5 h); Lisbon airport ~90 km.
orcaNotes: Offshore Centro Portugal orca zone in season.
notes: Best jumping-off point for the Berlengas. Good shelter, 20 visitor berths, laundry. World-class surf (Supertubos). 15 m LOA limit restricts larger yachts.

name: Berlengas - Carreiro do Mosteiro (anchorage)
approachNotes: Main anchorage in the Carreiro do Mosteiro on the SE side of Berlenga Grande, in the lee of the Fort of Sao Joao Baptista. The fort dominates the only navigable approach. Archipelago 10-17 km W of Peniche. Numerous reefs/islets - approach only in settled conditions with up-to-date chart. In any N-NW swell the anchorage is untenable. Anchor in sand. Farilhoes-Forcadas islets (NW) completely off-limits.
swellSensitivity: high - exposed to N-NW; only comfortable in E-SE/calm
bestTideEntry: Settled weather, near slack; never in NW swell
nearestTown: Peniche (10-17 km by sea/ferry); no provisioning on island.
notes: NATURE RESERVE (Decree 19/2022): landing requires Berlengaspass registration/fee (berlengaspass.icnf.pt); island visitor cap 550. Overnight anchorage needs pre-authorisation (contact ICNF). Spectacular in settled summer conditions only.

name: Marina de Cascais
approachNotes: N shore of Cascais Bay, 9 nm SE of Cabo da Roca. From N stay 1 nm off Cabo da Roca and Cabo Raso. Leave the three S cardinal buoys and a red can to port along the S breakwater. Entrance runs NE-SW; reception dock on N side. Min depth 6 m. External pontoon N of breakwater takes 50 m LOA. VHF 09. Approach boisterous in strong W-SW - arrive before conditions deteriorate.
swellSensitivity: low-moderate - protected in bay; approach rough in strong W-SW
nearestTown: Cascais (2 min) - full marina village; rail to Lisbon Cais do Sodre (40 min); Lisbon airport 35 km.
orcaNotes: Cabo da Roca / Cascais approaches have had orca interactions in season.
notes: 'Home of international sailing in Portugal'. 70 t travel hoist, repairs, dry storage. 125 visitor berths - pre-book in high season. Customs available. Natural arrival point for Lisbon / rounding Cabo da Roca.

name: Porto de Recreio de Oeiras
approachNotes: Mouth of the Tagus, 0.5 nm E of Sao Juliao da Barra fortress (distinctive tower landmark). Approach from SW following the S bank of the Tagus. No significant bar/swell - the Tagus mouth shelters from the Atlantic. Range 14 nm. Diesel + 95 unleaded; 100 dry-storage; pump-out.
swellSensitivity: low - sheltered inside Tagus mouth
nearestTown: Oeiras (1.5 km) - prosperous suburb; Cascais-line rail to Lisbon Cais do Sodre 25 min; Lisbon airport 20 km.
notes: Excellent all-round marina 8 nm up the Tagus, 15 nm from central Lisbon; more sheltered/quieter than Cascais.

name: Doca do Bom Sucesso (Belem)
approachNotes: Former Navy dock, just E of the Torre de Belem (UNESCO landmark). Call Porto de Lisboa VHF 12/74 approaching the Tagus, then VHF 09 for Bom Sucesso. Tagus approach wide and well-marked; pass Forte de Sao Juliao (S bank) and Bugio fort (mid-river sandbank). Strong Tagus ebb at springs. Max LOA 15 m.
swellSensitivity: low - inside Tagus
nearestTown: Belem, Lisbon (immediate - Jeronimos Monastery, Padrao dos Descobrimentos); Belem rail (Cascais line) 15 min to centre; airport 20 min.
notes: Most culturally situated marina - beside the Tower of Belem. 15 m LOA limit. Fuel on dock; pre-book advisable.

name: Doca de Belem (Porto de Lisboa)
approachNotes: Just upriver from Doca do Bom Sucesso; same Tagus approach. Strong tidal flow on Tagus springs; eddies at dock entrances. Contact VHF 09 (initial Porto de Lisboa VHF 12/74). Fuel on dock; workshop services.
swellSensitivity: low - inside Tagus
nearestTown: Belem (immediate - Jeronimos, MAAT); Belem rail 15 min to centre; airport 20 min.
notes: Very similar to Bom Sucesso, 500 m upriver. 15 m LOA limit. Mechanical hauling/workshop on site.

name: Doca de Alcantara (Porto de Lisboa)
approachNotes: First Lisbon marina from the sea (6 nm upriver), beside the Ponte 25 de Abril bridge. Initial call Porto de Lisboa VHF 12/74. If a cruise ship is at the adjacent quay, call the bridge operator VHF 68 for passage. Large commercial pier nearby - freighter traffic. Strong Tagus current on springs. Max draft 7 m. No fuel here (use Cascais/Oeiras/Belem). Immigration office on site. Low-frequency bridge hum reported.
swellSensitivity: very low - deep inside Tagus estuary
nearestTown: Alcantara / Bairro Alto (20-30 min walk to centre); Alcantara-Terra rail (Cascais line) 10 min to Cais do Sodre; airport 25 min.
notes: Best choice for large draft (7 m) in Lisbon; first available berth upriver. Customs/immigration clearance possible. No fuel - plan accordingly.

name: Marina Parque das Nacoes (Lisbon East)
approachNotes: Easternmost/furthest-upriver Lisbon marina. VHF 09/16. Galp fuel on site. Double-sluice lock isolates vessels from Tagus tidal range (constant level). Max draft 2.4 m (RESTRICTIVE - deeper vessels use Alcantara/Cascais). Max LOA 25 m (river pier to ~230 m for superyachts). Travel lift, crane, nautical shop.
swellSensitivity: very low - deep inside Tagus
nearestTown: Parque das Nacoes (modern district; Vasco da Gama mall 5 min); Oriente intercity rail hub 15 min walk; Lisbon airport 5 min by car.
notes: Best crew-change marina in Lisbon (5 min from airport, walk to Oriente rail). Galp fuel. 2.4 m draft limit restricts deeper keels. Largest Lisbon-area marina (600 berths).

name: Seixal Bay (Tagus South Bank anchorage)
approachNotes: Free anchorage on the S bank of the Tagus, opposite Alcantara. Anchor in mud in Baia do Seixal (mouth of the Rio Judeu). Good shelter from most directions but exposed to strong N winds channelling down the Tagus. Good holding. Cheap mooring buoys may be available from a dock cabin. Dinghy dock; frequent ferries to Lisbon Cais do Sodre (17-20 min).
swellSensitivity: low - well inside Tagus
nearestTown: Seixal town (500 m); minimarket; ferry to Lisbon 17 min.
notes: Budget option for exploring Lisbon - free anchoring + fast cheap ferry. Calm and quiet vs the Lisbon docas. Shallow bay - check tide/draft.

name: Marina Clube Naval de Sesimbra
approachNotes: Marina at the SE end of the fishing-harbour breakwater. Approach straightforward once the long SE breakwater is identified; give the breakwater end clearance (fishing boats exit fast). Call VHF 09/12 (16 calling). Fuel is in the fishing harbour (not the marina). Good anchorage in sand off the beach, open E-SE; bay sheltered from prevailing NW by Serra da Arrabida cliffs; ~70 summer mooring buoys.
swellSensitivity: low-moderate - sheltered from NW; SE swell enters bay
nearestTown: Sesimbra (200 m) - whitewashed fishing town, fish restaurants; buses to Setubal (30 min)/Lisbon (1 h); Lisbon airport 38 km.
notes: One of the most attractive stops on the route; excellent shelter from prevailing summer NW under the Arrabida cliffs; good value in winter. 15 m LOA limit. Good anchorage alternative.

name: Doca de Recreio das Fontainhas (Setubal)
approachNotes: Head of the Sado estuary, 40 km S of Lisbon by sea. Enter between the Troia Peninsula (N) and Outao headland (S). Approach buoy No.2 then ~040 for 3 nm toward Outao fort. At night Doca and Azeda lights form a leading line. Watch sandbanks on the Troia (NW) side. Resident bottlenose dolphins in the Sado. Marina split E (170 berths, <=10 m, 2.5 m) and W (150 berths, <=15 m, 2.5 m). Ferry pier opposite - beware wash/traffic. VHF 16.
swellSensitivity: low - inside Sado estuary
nearestTown: Setubal (500 m) - full city, fish market; rail IC to Lisbon Oriente 1 h; airport 1 h.
notes: Gateway to Arrabida Natural Park (limestone cliffs, anchoring off Arrabida beaches) and the Sado (dolphins). Troia Marina (across the estuary) is a smarter, pricier alternative. 15 m LOA limit (W side).

name: Marina de Sines
approachNotes: Marina entrance 1.5 nm S of Cabo de Sines; a 1700 m N-S breakwater protects it. From N beware a submerged outer breakwater section marked by a red buoy - do NOT pass inside it. Leave the red buoy to port. Dominant NW summer winds; winter strong S-SW. Some early-morning fog Jul-Oct. Deep anchorage area inside the breakwater. 45 t travel lift; 5 t crane. Customs available.
swellSensitivity: low - long breakwater; S swell may surge in winter
nearestTown: Sines (1 km, Vasco da Gama's birthplace); rail poor (Santiago do Cacem 15 km); buses to Lisbon 2.5 h; car rental recommended.
orcaNotes: Offshore SW Alentejo approaching Cabo de Sao Vicente sees rising orca risk; high around the cape.
notes: Primary staging post for Atlantic/Med passages - best shelter between Setubal and the Algarve; waiting point before rounding Cabo de Sao Vicente. 8 m max draft suits blue-water yachts. Customs for non-EU. Inside the vast Sines industrial port complex but well separated.
